root_cause.text: The `ssl3_get_cert_verify` function incorrectly allowed clients to skip the `CertificateVerify` message if their provided certificate lacked signing capabilities (such as a DH certificate), instead of strictly requiring the verify message whenever any client certificate was presented.
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes the `&& (type & EVP_PKT_SIGN)` condition in `ssl/s3_srvr.c`, ensuring that the `SSL_R_MISSING_VERIFY_MESSAGE` error is triggered anytime a client certificate is present (`peer != NULL`) but the verify message is missing, regardless of the certificate's type.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `ssl3_get_cert_verify` function incorrectly allowed clients to skip the `CertificateVerify` message if their provided certificate lacked signing capabilities (such as a DH certificate), instead of strictly requiring the verify message whenever any client certificate was presented.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch removes the `&& (type & EVP_PKT_SIGN)` condition in `ssl/s3_srvr.c`, ensuring that the `SSL_R_MISSING_VERIFY_MESSAGE` error is triggered anytime a client certificate is present (`peer != NULL`) but the verify message is missing, regardless of the certificate's type.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix a vulnerability by strictly enforcing the presence of a CertificateVerify message when a peer certificate is provided.
intent_record.what: Removed the check for EVP_PKT_SIGN from the conditional statement.
intent_record.how: It enforces the error path whenever a peer certificate is present, preventing the bypass of the CertificateVerify check.

grouping_result.intent: Fix a vulnerability by strictly enforcing the presence of a CertificateVerify message when a peer certificate is provided.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by strictly enforcing the presence of the CertificateVerify message whenever a peer certificate is provided, fixing the logic flaw described in the root cause.

candidates.rationale: This line incorrectly allowed skipping the CertificateVerify message by checking for signing capabilities (type & EVP_PKT_SIGN) instead of strictly checking for the presence of a peer certificate.

vuln_statements.explanation: This condition incorrectly allows a client to skip the CertificateVerify message if the certificate lacks signing capabilities, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 3b1fb1a0226e29c9d7c79ff7fbde21ef9cac4deb
Author: Andy Polyakov <appro@openssl.org>
Date:   Sun Apr 15 17:22:57 2012 +0000

    s3_srvr.c: fix typo.
    PR: 2538

diff --git a/ssl/s3_srvr.c b/ssl/s3_srvr.c
index ebf432421f..87678c181b 100644
--- a/ssl/s3_srvr.c
+++ b/ssl/s3_srvr.c
@@ -2980,7 +2980,7 @@ int ssl3_get_cert_verify(SSL *s)
 	if (s->s3->tmp.message_type != SSL3_MT_CERTIFICATE_VERIFY)
 		{
 		s->s3->tmp.reuse_message=1;
-		if ((peer != NULL) && (type | EVP_PKT_SIGN))
+		if ((peer
bic_trace_step.reason: The code checks `(peer != NULL) && (type & EVP_PKT_SIGN)` which allows skipping the CertificateVerify message if the certificate lacks signing capabilities. The vulnerability root cause states that the verify message should be strictly required whenever ANY client certificate is presented, meaning the check for signing capabilities is the bug itself. Therefore, the bug is present and not fixed by this commit.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Import of old SSLeay release: SSLeay 0.8.1b
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit d02b48c63a58ea4367a0e905979f140b7d090f86
Author: Ralf S. Engelschall <rse@openssl.org>
Date:   Mon Dec 21 10:52:47 1998 +0000

    Import of old SSLeay release: SSLeay 0.8.1b

diff --git a/ssl/s3_srvr.c b/ssl/s3_srvr.c
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..ebff57506f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/ssl/s3_srvr.c
@@ -0,0 +1,1629 @@
+/* ssl/s3_srvr.c */
+/* Copyright (C) 1995-1997 Eric Young (eay@cryptsoft.com)
+ * All rights reserved.
+ *
+ * This package is an SSL implementation written
+ * by Eric
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `ssl3_get_cert_verify` attempts to conditionally require the CertificateVerify message based on the certificate type using `if ((peer != NULL) && (type | EVP_PKT_SIGN))` (note the bitwise OR typo which likely was intended to be AND). This conditional check rather than strictly requiring the verify message whenever `peer != NULL` is the root cause of the vulnerability.
